channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-18
item.description:
This week, our open-source research community saw exciting updates across multiple projects. The **scienceverse/metacheck** and **giladfeldman/escicheck** teams made notable progress, with metacheck's 'main' branch receiving a fix for issue #314 and escicheck's main branch being synchronized with effectcheck version 0.6.3. Meanwhile, **Davidvandijcke/coarse** made significant strides in localization, adding support for multiple languages and refining site-UI wording. Additionally, **shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve** and **giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward** saw updates, including a revised validation note with a citation and the addition of a Chrome Webstore link, respectively. These updates demonstrate the community's commitment to collaboration and improvement, and we celebrate the connections between these projects as they continue to advance open-source research tools.

119 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck33 commits
docpluck32 commits
metacheck27 commits
coarse24 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 5 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The 'main' branch was updated to fix issue #314 by updating the metacheck page. The 'dev' branch saw a change in the Get Started vignette, where 'create_report_app()' was fixed to 'report_app()'. The 'ethics_check' branch had an 'ethics_check' added along with a bug fix. The 'new_modules' branch initially had a fix for app instruction #314 reverted, but this revert was then itself reverted, effectively fixing the app instruction #314.

5 commit(s) today:

  • 25c15a6 [main] update metacheck page to fix #314 — Lakens
  • 73736b0 [dev] Fix create_report_app() to report_app() in Get Started vignette — Daniel Lakens
  • 00d7953 [ethics_check] add ethics_check + bug fix — Lakens
  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens
  • 1d3719b [new_modules] fix app instruction #314 — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with three commits made to it. The first commit, "docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary)", indicates that a handoff was run, version 2.4.92 was shipped, the caption guard was reverted, and work was paused at an architectural boundary. Another commit, "docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal", added documentation for cycle 2, which includes the affiliation guard for version 2.4.92, and cycle 3, where the caption-follows feature was reverted. A feature commit, "feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92)", changed the rendering to never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings, specifically for version 2.4.92.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 2fd8711 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary) — Gilad Feldman
  • c671929 [main] docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal — Gilad Feldman
  • cf420ca [main] feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 19 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the 'dev' branch, as well as several feature branches including 'feat/i18n-setup-compare', 'feat/237-lang-eval', 'feat/nl-flemish', 'feat/i18n-status-review-pages', 'feat/231-site-i18n', 'feat/229-i18n-labels', and 'feat/226-i18n-deenglish'. The 'dev' branch and 'feat/i18n-setup-compare' branch saw the addition of localization for setup and compare pages, which now follow the site language, as stated in the commit message "feat(i18n): localize setup + compare pages; follow site language". The 'feat/nl-flemish' and 'dev' branches included Dutch site-UI wording refinements, with the commit message specifying "i18n(nl): Dutch site-UI wording refinements". The 'dev' branch also saw the localization of status and review pages, with the addition of Opus QA for all 12 catalogs, as described in the commit message "feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs". Additionally, the 'dev' branch and 'feat/231-site-i18n' branch included the implementation of a localized website UI with a manual language switcher, as stated in the commit message "feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H)".

19 commit(s) today:

  • 2e1c796 [dev] feat(i18n): localize setup + compare pages; follow site language (#239) (#240) — David Van Dijcke
  • ef4f2eb [feat/i18n-setup-compare] feat(i18n): localize setup + compare pages; follow site language (#239) — Davidvandijcke
  • e8c3845 [dev] feat(i18n): cross-language review-consistency eval harness (PR-F) (#236) (#238) — David Van Dijcke
  • f1fbfe9 [dev] i18n(nl): Dutch site-UI wording refinements (#235) (#237) — David Van Dijcke
  • c3370c4 [feat/237-lang-eval] feat(i18n): cross-language review-consistency eval harness (PR-F) (#236) — Davidvandijcke
  • 228b694 [feat/nl-flemish] i18n(nl): Dutch site-UI wording refinements (#235) — Davidvandijcke
  • 1910472 [dev] feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs (#233) (#234) — David Van Dijcke
  • 9ac6256 [feat/i18n-status-review-pages] feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs (#233) — Davidvandijcke
  • 8faa90a [dev] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) (#232) — David Van Dijcke
  • 395a58c [feat/231-site-i18n] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) — Davidvandijcke
  • eae44c1 [dev] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) (#230) — David Van Dijcke
  • db35eb5 [feat/229-i18n-labels] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) — Davidvandijcke
  • ef7b339 [dev] feat(i18n): de-English-ify the analysis (PR-C) (#226) (#228) — David Van Dijcke
  • 3117681 [dev] feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2) (#223) (#225) — David Van Dijcke
  • 8510764 [feat/226-i18n-deenglish] feat(i18n): de-English-ify the analysis (PR-C) (#226) — Davidvandijcke
  • a12f9dc [dev] feat(web): add GLM 5.2 to the model picker (#227) — David Van Dijcke

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 11 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, with multiple commits made to it. The first commit "harden json handling" suggests that the JSON handling in the repository was made more robust. A fix was then applied to address an "oss bug", as stated in the corresponding commit message. Additionally, the storage mechanism was modified to "use more efficient storage", increasing the overall performance. Finally, a patch was applied to the "oss SDK handler" to resolve any existing issues with it.

11 commit(s) today:

  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The main branch was worked on in this set of commits. The commit message indicates that the repository was "sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3)" which suggests that changes from the escicheckapp repository, specifically from the commit f52d7ce, were synchronized into the main branch, bringing it up to date with effectcheck version 0.6.3. This sync is associated with issue #17. No other branches were mentioned in the provided commits. The synchronization brought the main branch in line with the specified version of effectcheck.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was worked on, where a Chrome Webstore link was added for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The addition of the Chrome Webstore link is the sole change made in this update. There are no new features, fixes, or modules added aside from this link. The commit message directly mentions the addition of this link, indicating its purpose. No other code modifications were included in this commit.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
